# Checklist — Software Testing (SWEBOK v4, Chapter 5)

> Reference checklist derived from **SWEBOK Guide v4.0, Chapter 05 — Software Testing** (pp. 5-1 → 5-35).
> Each row defines one verification check that should be answered **at requirements time** (interview / SRS authoring), so that the SRS captures everything the team needs to test the resulting system effectively.
> **Ordered by severity of a *missing* check** (Critical → High → Medium → Low). See severity scale in [checklist-software-requirements.md](checklist-software-requirements.md).

## Checklist

| ID | Check name | Reference (§ / page) | Severity | What must be verified | Concrete example (✅ success / ❌ failure) |
|----|------------|----------------------|----------|----------------------|--------------------------------------------|
| ST-001 | Every functional requirement has a usable test oracle | §1.2.7 The Oracle Problem `[5-4, 5-5]` | Critical | For every functional requirement, the expected outcome is decidable (unambiguous spec, behavioural model, code annotation, or formal acceptance criteria). No "we'll know it when we see it" requirements. | ✅ "Given a valid PIN and balance ≥ amount, when the user requests withdrawal, then the cash is dispensed AND balance debited AND a receipt printed" — pass/fail is mechanical. ❌ "The system shall handle transactions correctly" — no oracle, no pass/fail. |
| ST-002 | Acceptance criteria defined before implementation (ATDD/BDD) | §2.1.4 Acceptance Testing `[5-7]`; SR KA §4.3 | Critical | Each user-visible feature has acceptance tests (ATDD test cases or BDD Given/When/Then) agreed by engineers + domain experts + QA **before** production code is written. At least one acceptance test fails on the existing software prior to implementation. | ✅ Story "withdraw cash" has 4 BDD scenarios (sufficient balance, insufficient balance, disabled card, ATM empty) reviewed by business + dev + QA, all failing on baseline. ❌ Acceptance criteria written after dev finished — they merely describe what was built. |
| ST-003 | Compliance scope (laws/regulations) explicitly captured | §2.2.2 Compliance Testing `[5-8]` | Critical | All laws/regulations the SUT must adhere to (GDPR, HIPAA, PCI-DSS, DO-178C, ISO 26262, EN 50128, IEC 62304, etc.) are listed, and each generates compliance requirements with traceable acceptance evidence. | ✅ SRS §1.4 lists "GDPR Art. 5/15/17/30, ISO/IEC 27001 Annex A.8/A.12"; each obligation traces to one or more REQs and to compliance test cases. ❌ Spec ignores GDPR; audit raises a finding on the data-export feature 3 months after release. |
| ST-004 | Security testing scope agreed (CIA + misuse/abuse) | §2.2.8 Security Testing `[5-9]`; Security KA §4.5 | Critical | The spec states which security properties (Confidentiality / Integrity / Availability) will be validated and which misuse/abuse cases must be exercised. Pen-test scope authorized in writing. | ✅ SRS §6.3: "Authorisation matrix RBAC-001 → test cases TC-SEC-014…022; misuse cases MU-01 (forced browsing), MU-02 (privilege escalation), MU-03 (replay)." Pen-test SOW approved. ❌ Security mentioned only as "must be secure"; no misuse cases; no auth for pen-test. |
| ST-005 | Privacy testing scope agreed (personal data) | §2.2.9 Privacy Testing `[5-9, 5-10]` | Critical | Personal/sensitive data, applicable privacy policies, and decentralised-storage validations are explicitly identified and assigned acceptance criteria. | ✅ Data classification table marks "DoB, email, IP" as PII; privacy acceptance criteria PR-01..PR-07 validate consent, export, erasure, audit logging. ❌ Privacy assumed implicit; team ships an export feature that returns other users' addresses. |
| ST-006 | Boundary / equivalence / exception scenarios elicited | §3.1.2 Boundary Value Analysis `[5-11]`; §3.1.11 Forcing Exception `[5-12]` | Critical | For each input or state, the spec captures equivalence classes (valid / invalid) and boundary values; explicit exceptions / negative scenarios listed (overflow, underflow, timeouts, protection, data exception). | ✅ "Deposit amount: equivalence classes {0, 0.01..1M, >1M}; boundaries 0/0.01/999 999.99/1 000 000.00/1 000 000.01; exception scenarios: NaN, negative, currency mismatch." ❌ Only "happy path" deposit story; QA finds overflow at scale 6 weeks later. |
| ST-007 | Non-functional acceptance numbers quantified | §2.2.7 Non-Functional Testing `[5-8, 5-9]`; SR KA §3.1, §3.2 | Critical | Performance, load, stress, volume, failover, scalability, elasticity, reliability targets are stated numerically with units (and ideally with fail and perfection points). | ✅ "p95 login latency ≤ 300 ms at 1 000 RPS sustained 10 min; degrade gracefully ≤ 1 s under 3× peak; auto-scale ≤ 90 s." ❌ "Must be fast and scalable." |
| ST-008 | Test data and operational profile available or planned | §3.5.1 Operational Profile `[5-15]`; §1.2.15 Online vs Offline `[5-6]` | High | An operational profile (usage frequency distribution) or representative test data set is defined, so reliability / load / stress tests reflect real usage. Source of test data identified (anonymised production / synthetic / fixtures). | ✅ Profile: "Read 92% / Write 7% / Admin 1%; peak 18:00–20:00 weekdays; 10k MAU"; anonymised dump pipeline approved by DPO. ❌ Load tests run with 100 identical GET requests; production crashes on the first write burst. |
| ST-009 | Required test levels are explicit (unit/integration/system/acceptance) | §2.1 Target of the Test `[5-6, 5-7]` | High | The SRS or test strategy identifies which test levels are mandatory and which subsystems each level targets — especially the **integration boundary** to external systems and hardware. | ✅ Test strategy table: each component lists U/I/S/A coverage and which external interface is exercised at integration level. ❌ Team unit-tests only; integration with payment gateway is "tested in prod". |
| ST-010 | Each requirement is testable (quantified, with measurement method) | §1.2.10 Testability `[5-5]`; SR KA §3.1 | High | Compliance/non-compliance is mechanically demonstrable. The measurement method, instrumentation needs, and tools are identified for non-trivial measures. | ✅ "Search p95 ≤ 800 ms — measured via Grafana p95 dashboard for `/api/search` over a rolling 5 min window under the operational profile." ❌ "Search must be responsive." |
| ST-011 | Interface (incl. API) contracts specify data exchange precisely | §2.2.10 Interface and API Testing `[5-10]`; SR KA §5.2 | High | All inter-component interfaces and external APIs have explicit message formats, parameters, ranges, error codes, and behaviours under malformed input. | ✅ OpenAPI 3.1 doc + examples + error catalog covering 4xx/5xx; contract tests in CI. ❌ API doc says "POST /orders — see code"; integration team guesses payload. |
| ST-012 | Configuration variants are enumerated and supported | §2.2.11 Configuration Testing `[5-10]` | High | All supported configurations (browsers, OS versions, hardware, locales, feature flags, deployment topologies) are listed with the matrix the system must work in. | ✅ Browser matrix: Chrome 122/Edge 122/Safari 17/Firefox 124 × Win11/macOS 14/iOS 17/Android 14. ❌ "Must work on modern browsers" — Safari users find layout broken. |
| ST-013 | Compatibility & coexistence requirements stated | §2.2.7 Compatibility `[5-9]`; SR KA §2.3 | High | The other software/hardware the SUT must coexist with (and the version range) are listed; migration & rollback paths are specified. | ✅ "Coexists with billing-svc ≥ 4.2 < 5.0 sharing Postgres `customer` schema; rollback path = `flyway undo` to V42." ❌ Deploy of v5 breaks billing-svc; no rollback was specified. |
| ST-014 | Recovery / failover requirements stated | §2.2.7 Failover / Recovery `[5-9]` | High | Allowed downtime, RTO/RPO, failover triggers and procedures, and crash-recovery requirements are part of the spec. | ✅ "Single-AZ failure → RTO ≤ 60 s, RPO = 0 (sync replication). Full-region failure → RTO ≤ 30 min, RPO ≤ 5 min." ❌ "Should be highly available." |
| ST-015 | Coverage criterion against requirements stated | §4 Test-Related Measures `[5-16]`; SR KA §7.3 | High | A coverage measure tying tests to requirements is defined (e.g., 100% of explicitly-prioritised functional requirements have at least one acceptance test linked). | ✅ Traceability matrix shows 100% of "Must" requirements have ≥ 1 passing acceptance test before release. ❌ 38% of Must requirements have no linked test at GA. |
| ST-016 | Regression test scope agreed | §2.2.5 Regression Testing `[5-8]` | High | The SRS / test strategy declares which test suite acts as regression gate, what triggers it (per-PR / nightly / per-release), and which requirements are protected by it. | ✅ "PR gate: unit + contract; nightly: full E2E + load smoke; release: full E2E + perf + security baseline." ❌ Regression is whatever QA happens to re-run that day. |
| ST-017 | Domain-specific testing constraints captured | §3.6 Techniques by Nature of Application `[5-15, 5-16]` | Medium | The nature of the SUT (real-time, embedded, ML-based, safety-critical, mobile, cloud, blockchain, …) and the **mandatory domain standards** (ISO 26262, DO-178C, EN 50128, IEC 62304, IEC 61508, ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119) are noted, since they constrain test techniques. | ✅ "ML model is safety-relevant; metamorphic + adversarial robustness tests required; ISO 21448 (SOTIF) applies." ❌ ML model accepted purely on accuracy; first adversarial example causes wrong classification in prod. |
| ST-018 | Pen-test legal authorisation in place before any dynamic security test | §4.5 Security Testing `[13-5]` | Medium | Penetration / vulnerability tests are authorised in writing within legal boundaries; targets, time windows, allowed techniques and data handling are agreed. | ✅ Signed pen-test rules of engagement covering scope, timing, allowed techniques (no DoS), data handling, escalation contacts. ❌ Tester runs sqlmap against prod without authorisation; company faces legal exposure. |
| ST-019 | Usability acceptance criteria defined | §2.2.12 Usability & HCI Testing `[5-10]`; SR KA §6.1 | Medium | Usability is acceptance-checked with concrete tasks, success rate, completion time, satisfaction metric (e.g., SUS). | ✅ "5 representative users complete tasks T1..T5 unassisted within 2 min each; SUS ≥ 75." ❌ "User-friendly UI" with no measurement. |
| ST-020 | Alpha/beta release strategy planned (if applicable) | §2.2.4 Alpha & Beta Testing `[5-8]` | Medium | If the product life cycle includes alpha/beta, the participating users, feedback channels, support level and exit criteria are defined. | ✅ Beta with 200 invited users, in-app feedback, weekly triage, exit on crash-free sessions ≥ 99.5% and ≤ 5 P1 open. ❌ Beta released to anyone; no feedback channel; team can't tell whether to GA. |
| ST-021 | Static analysis / code review obligations stated as requirements | §1.2.6 Testing for QA / Improvement `[5-4]`; Security KA §5.1 | Medium | The SRS or constitution requires SAST / DAST / SCA gates and review obligations (e.g., 2 reviewers, security review for crypto changes). | ✅ "PRs blocked on SAST high findings; SCA blocks on CVSS ≥ 7; crypto changes require security-team review." ❌ Tooling exists but is advisory only; security findings backlog grows unbounded. |
| ST-022 | Test data and PII handling rules defined | §2.2.9 Privacy `[5-9, 5-10]` | Medium | The provenance, anonymisation, retention and disposal of test data (esp. PII) are specified to avoid privacy incidents in non-prod. | ✅ "Test DB seeded from prod via Faker; emails replaced with `+test@`; retained 30 days; refresh weekly." ❌ Prod export with real customer emails sits in QA env indefinitely. |
| ST-023 | Test environment fidelity and infrastructure assumptions declared | §2.2.3 Installation Testing `[5-8]`; §1.2.15 `[5-6]` | Medium | The test environment topology, infrastructure dependencies and how they differ from production are stated; an installation/upgrade test is planned. | ✅ "Staging = 1× replica of prod region, smaller node sizes; install/upgrade test runs every release." ❌ Staging uses SQLite while prod uses Postgres; first prod migration fails. |
| ST-024 | Combinatorial test obligations for high-arity configurations | §3.1.4 Combinatorial Testing / OAT `[5-11]` | Medium | When the SUT has many independent parameters (≥ 4 with multiple values each), the spec requires pair-wise / OAT coverage rather than all-combinations. | ✅ Feature flags × roles × locales × tenants matrix is tested with pair-wise (32 cases instead of 384). ❌ Team only tests "default" config; first customer combination triggers an unseen bug. |
| ST-025 | State-transition behaviour modelled when behaviour is modal | §3.1.7 State Transition Testing `[5-12]` | Medium | Requirements involving modes/states (e.g., order lifecycle, device states, workflow stages) include the state machine or table; transitions and disallowed events are specified. | ✅ Order spec includes a state diagram (`draft → submitted → paid → shipped → closed`) with allowed events per state. ❌ Order workflow described in prose; backend allows `shipped → submitted` regression bug. |
| ST-026 | Reliability target stated (with growth model commitment) | §4.1.4 / 4.1.5 Reliability `[5-17, 5-18]` | Medium | Reliability goals are stated (e.g., MTBF, failure rate per unit work) with the model the team will use to track them. | ✅ "Target: ≤ 1 P1 incident / 30 days; track with Goel-Okumoto growth model on integration test failures." ❌ "Highly reliable" with no metric, no model, no tracking. |
| ST-027 | Test data volume sufficient for volume testing | §2.2.7 Volume Testing `[5-9]` | Low | Spec includes the upper bound on data volumes (rows, files, message size) the SUT must process and how it degrades beyond that. | ✅ "Catalog up to 10M SKUs; search must remain ≤ 1 s p95 at 10M; degraded mode > 10M with clear error." ❌ Unbounded growth assumed; system OOMs at 2M rows. |
| ST-028 | Back-to-back / shadow testing planned for replacements | §2.2.7 Back-to-Back `[5-9]` | Low | When the new SUT replaces an existing one, the spec calls for shadow / back-to-back comparison on real traffic before cutover. | ✅ "Shadow mode for 4 weeks; alert on > 0.1% output divergence." ❌ Hard cutover; subtle rounding difference flagged by customers after release. |
| ST-029 | Exploratory testing budget allocated | §3.3.2 Exploratory Testing `[5-13, 5-14]` | Low | The plan reserves dedicated time for exploratory testing by experienced testers, in addition to scripted acceptance tests, especially around new or risky features. | ✅ "1 day/sprint reserved for charters-based exploratory testing on the riskiest feature; findings logged." ❌ Only scripted tests run; subtle UX issues reach end users. |
| ST-030 | Test process artefacts under configuration management | §1.2.5 Assessment & Certification `[5-4]`; §5 Test Process `[5-18]` | Low | Test plans, test cases, test data, test results and tooling versions are under CM so executions are repeatable and auditable. | ✅ All test artefacts in Git; CI run IDs link back to commit + tool versions; certifications consume the same artefacts. ❌ Test cases live on testers' laptops; "it passed yesterday" but nobody can reproduce. |
